figures.caption: Fig. 2: Object-centered spherical coordinate mapping and coverage update workflow. The baseline rotation R0 = R(q(0)) is obtained from the initial quaternion q(0). During data acquisition, the device records quaternion q(t), acceleration a(t), and angular velocity ω(t); after IMU-based stability gate, the rotation matrix R(q(t)) and relative rotation Rrel(t) = R⊤ 0 R(t) are derived. The camera forward vector ez = [0, 0, 1]⊤ is transformed to the object frame as v(t) = Rrel(t)ez and converted into spherical coordinates θ and ϕ. Each stable capture is mapped to a sampling direction (p, t) on the spherical grid, with the corresponding cell C(p, t) used to update the area-weighted coverage in real time.

Table 2: Band-wise comparison of image counts (Img.) and coverage (Cov.) between free capture and our method for Coinbank. Avg. denotes the average of free capture. Left to right: front (−45◦ to 45◦), side, back, and opposite side.
